critiques.summary: Path-aware review of the whole set via GitHub Copilot. Read path_aware_critique.py, close_session.py, start_session.py, the artifact validator and gate, the tests, and the schema docs. Reproduced each finding against the shipped code. Three findings: a corrupt activity-log silently disarms the gate, the gate accepts a stale/cross-set artifact, and the test slice does not cover either failure mode. Raw verdict saved at s3-path-aware-critique-gpt-5.4.md.

findings.description: The durable policy can be silently downgraded to 'none' by an unreadable/corrupt activity-log.json: read_path_aware_critique() and has_path_aware_critique_record() both swallow OSError/JSONDecodeError and return the default, and close_session gates on pac_level != none before calling the gate, so a broken log skips the Set 066 gate entirely. Reproduced: invalid activity-log.json -> read=='none', has_record==False, gate applicable=False/ok=True. Fix: represent 'record unreadable' as a distinct state the close path treats as a gate problem/warning, not an implicit opt-out.

The runtime gate treats sessionSetName and the artifact's pathAwareCritique as decorative. validate_path_aware_critique_artifact only checks sessionSetName is non-empty and pathAwareCritique is in the enum; validate_path_aware_critique_gate never compares either to the active set name or recorded level. Reproduced: a set recorded 'required' passed the gate with an artifact whose sessionSetName was 'some-other-set' and pathAwareCritique was 'advisory'. A copied/stale artifact from another set can satisfy a required gate. Fix: compare artifact sessionSetName to Path(session_set_dir).name and its pathAwareCritique to the recorded level before accepting.

The test slice (91 tests) gives a stronger sense of coverage than it earns: none exercise the two failure modes above (corrupt/unreadable activity-log; a structurally valid artifact whose sessionSetName/pathAwareCritique do not match the active set). Fix: add explicit regression tests for unreadable durable-record handling and wrong-set/wrong-level artifact rejection.

critiques.summary: Path-aware review via GitHub Copilot with repo access. Verified claims 1-3 and 7 in path_aware_critique.py, claims 4-6 in close_session.py, claim 1 in start_session.py, claims 8-9 in blast_radius.py, claim 2 against the JSON Schema, claim 12 in pyproject.toml, and claim 10 in this set's spec.md/activity-log.json. The immutability path, distinct-provider rule, terminal-close scoping, fail-open import guard, net-new wiring, tier-orthogonality, and dogfood arming all hold. Two validator/schema parity gaps found. Raw verdict saved at path-aware-critique-gemini.md.

findings.description: The pure-Python validator does not type-check optional fields the JSON Schema constrains: critiquedAt (schema: string), blastRadius (schema: object), and a Finding's severity/category (schema: strings). An artifact with arbitrary types (e.g. an integer critiquedAt or a string blastRadius) passes the Python validator at close-out but fails strict JSON Schema evaluation. Fix: add isinstance checks for these optional properties when present.

The JSON Schema expects schemaVersion to be an integer, but the Python validator checks `version not in (1,)`; because Python treats 1.0 == 1 (and True == 1), an artifact with schemaVersion 1.0 or True silently passes the Python gate while failing strict JSON Schema. Fix: enforce isinstance(version, int) and not isinstance(version, bool).
